commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer config/security contract review remain; automation should not attempt repair because the external real-setup proof must come from the contributor or maintainer environment.; Needs attention: The latest code no longer shows the earlier session-global boundary bug, but the new authenticated-node tool boundary still lacks accepted real-setup proof and operator documentation.; Rev... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `gateway.tools.byNode`, threads an authenticated run-scoped `hostingNodeId` from node-originated agent requests into embedded agent runs, and filters the embedded tool set by that node's allow/deny policy.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +150. Total +282 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: the latest PR head adds `gateway.tools.byNode` to the schema, while current main's config help and public gateway docs still document only `gateway.tools.allow` and `gateway.tools.deny`. I did not independently reproduce the paired-node runtime proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer config/security contract review remain; automation should not attempt repair because the external real-setup proof must come from the contributor or maintainer environment.

target.title: feat(gateway): restrict session tools by authenticated hosting node
target.body:
## What

Adds `gateway.tools.byNode` — a per-node tool restriction keyed off the **authenticated** node hosting a turn, enforced **run-scoped**.

When a turn is dispatched from a node-originated `agent.request`, the gateway threads that node's id (from the node's cryptographic device pairing — a client cannot forge it) through the run as `hostingNodeId`. The in-process agent tool builder resolves the node's `allow`/`deny` from it and narrows that turn's toolset.

## Why

A node may legitimately drive agent turns, but those turns should be confined to the capabilities that node is trusted with. Existing tool policy is keyed off profiles/agents/senders/groups — none of which is an *authenticated node identity*. `byNode` fills that gap with a restriction that is sound to enforce (not merely advise), because the `nodeId` originates from the node's authenticated connection.

## How it works (run-scoped)

- The hosting node id is **carried with the run**, not looked up from a session-global map — so a node's policy can never bleed onto a later or concurrent turn for the same session.
- `server-node-events` passes `hostingNodeId` on the node-originated `agent.request` dispatch → `AgentCommandOpts` → `runEmbeddedAgent` (`RunEmbeddedAgentParams`) → `EmbeddedRunAttemptParams` → `createOpenClawCodingTools`, which calls `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(hostingNodeId, cfg)` (`node-tool-policy.ts`).
- Enforced in the **embedded agent tool builder** — the path a node-originated turn actually runs through. The MCP/HTTP scoped resolver (`resolveGatewayScopedTools`) is not node-originated, so it carries no hosting node → `byNode` is a no-op there.
- **Restriction-only**: it can narrow the toolset, never escalate. An explicitly-present (even empty) `allow` is **fail-closed**; `deny` extends the existing deny set; spawned subagents inherit the narrowed surface.
- Config: `gateway.tools.byNode` added to the TS type and the Zod schema (strict inner object).

## Testing

- `pnpm build`: clean.
- `node-tool-policy` unit tests — allow/deny resolution, fail-closed empty allow, deny-only, no-op when no hosting node, no-op when node unconfigured.
- Embedded-builder `byNode` tests (in the `createOpenClawCodingTools` suite) — assert the resolved toolset narrows to the node's allow / drops its deny, via the run-scoped `hostingNodeId`.
- Config round-trip — schema accepts `byNode`, rejects an unknown inner key.
- **Live behavior proof (real gateway + paired node, loopback):** a real authenticated node (auto-approved) emits a node-originated `agent.request` over its own connection; the gateway attributes it and applies `byNode`. Instrumented tool-resolution log on the node-driven turn:
  - `allow=[web_search,image]` → `before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (clean narrowing)
  - `allow=[browser,memory_search,memory_get]` (none present in that turn) → `after=0{}` (fail-closed)
  This exercises the full path end-to-end — node-authenticated `agent.request` → run-scoped `hostingNodeId` → `createOpenClawCodingTools` → `byNode` filter — in a disposable box.

## Risk / mitigation

- *Misconfiguration*: `byNode` can only **reduce** a node's tools, never grant — a bad entry fails safe. Covered by the no-escalate and fail-closed tests.
- *Cross-turn scoping*: the node id is run-scoped (threaded through the run), not a TTL'd session map, so it cannot apply to a later/concurrent turn for the same session.
